method: Add scan_perspectives field checking to check_fmea_audit.py. Detect single-perspective FMEA scans structurally. Retroactively tag existing FMEA artifacts with perspective metadata.
expect: FM-35 UNMITIGATED→MINIMAL with 1 automated defense layer (check_fmea_audit.py perspective diversity check). Check fires WARNING when <2 perspectives detected in last 3 FMEA artifacts.
actual: check_scan_perspectives() added to check_fmea_audit.py. Scans last 3 FMEA artifacts for scan_perspectives field. Fires NOTICE when field missing, WARNING when <2 perspectives. Retroactive tag added to f-cat1-fmea-s452.json. Test: WARNING correctly fires (1 perspective, need ≥2). FM-35 UNMITIGATED→MINIMAL (1 automated advisory layer).
diff: Matched expectation. The check is advisory (NOTICE/WARNING), not blocking — appropriate for meta-failure detection where false-positive blocking would reduce FMEA frequency. Future FMEA artifacts must include scan_perspectives field. Upgrade path to ADEQUATE: wire check into orient.py FMEA periodic DUE recommendation.

defense_layers.mechanism: Scan last 3 FMEA artifacts for scan_perspectives diversity. NOTICE if field missing, WARNING if <2 perspectives.

data.upgrade_path: Layer 2: wire into orient.py so FMEA-refresh DUE includes multi-perspective requirement. Layer 3: open_lane.py gate — new FMEA lanes must declare attention_frame at creation.
data.evidence: L-1108: 2 independent scans produced 0% overlap (8 FMs total, 4 per scan). Single-perspective underestimates by ~50%.
data.falsification: Falsified if next 3 FMEA refreshes with declared diverse perspectives produce ≤same FM count as single-perspective scans (i.e., perspective diversity doesn't improve yield).

verdict: FM-35 hardened UNMITIGATED→MINIMAL. Structural enforcement of multi-perspective FMEA scanning via check_fmea_audit.py. Advisory-level (appropriate for meta-failure — blocking would reduce FMEA frequency). Updated distribution: 0 INADEQUATE, 13 MINIMAL, 7 PARTIAL, 11 ADEQUATE, 6 UNMITIGATED (37 total).
